2603.23136.title: HGNet: Scalable Foundation Model for Automated Knowledge Graph Generation from Scientific Literature
2603.23136.paper_summary: Automated knowledge graph (KG) construction is essential for navigating the rapidly expanding body of scientific literature. However, existing approaches struggle to recognize long multi-word entities, often fail to generalize across domains, and typically overlook the hierarchical nature of scientific knowledge. While general-purpose large language models (LLMs) offer adaptability, they are computationally expensive and yield inconsistent accuracy on specialized tasks. As a result, current KGs are shallow and inconsistent, limiting their utility for exploration and synthesis. We propose a two-stage framework for scalable, zero-shot scientific KG construction. The first stage, Z-NERD, introduces (i) Orthogonal Semantic Decomposition (OSD), which promotes domain-agnostic entity recognition by isolating semantic "turns" in text, and (ii) a Multi-Scale TCQK attention mechanism that captures coherent multi-word entities through n-gram-aware attention heads. The second stage, HGNet, performs relation extraction with hierarchy-aware message passing, explicitly modeling parent, child, and peer relations. To enforce global consistency, we introduce two complementary objectives: a Differentiable Hierarchy Loss to discourage cycles and shortcut edges, and a Continuum Abstraction Field (CAF) Loss that embeds abstraction levels along a learnable axis in Euclidean space. This is the first approach to formalize hierarchical abstraction as a continuous property within standard Euclidean embeddings, offering a simpler alternative to hyperbolic methods. We release SPHERE (https://github.com/basiralab/SPHERE), a multi-domain benchmark for hierarchical relation extraction. Our framework establishes a new state of the art on SciERC, SciER, and SPHERE, improving NER by 8.08% and RE by 5.99% on out-of-distribution tests. In zero-shot settings, gains reach 10.76% for NER and 26.2% for RE.
2603.23136.paper_summary_zh: 自動化知識圖譜（KG）構建對於導航快速擴展的科學文獻體系至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有的方法在識別長的多詞實體方面存在困難，通常無法在不同領域之間進行泛化，並且通常忽略了科學知識的層級性質。<lb/>儘管通用的大型語言模型（LLMs）提供了適應性，但它們計算成本高昂，並且在專門任務上產生不一致的準確性。<lb/>因此，目前的知識圖譜淺薄且不一致，限制了它們在探索和綜合方面的實用性。<lb/>我們提出了一個兩階段的框架，用於可擴展的零樣本科學知識圖譜構建。<lb/>第一階段，Z-NERD，引入了（i）正交語義分解（OSD），通過隔離文本中的語義“轉折”來促進與領域無關的實體識別，以及（ii）一種多尺度TCQK注意力機制，通過n-gram感知的注意力頭捕捉連貫的多詞實體。<lb/>第二階段，HGNet，通過層級感知的消息傳遞執行關係提取，明確建模父級、子級和同級關係。<lb/>為了強化全局一致性，我們引入了兩個互補目標：一個可微分的層級損失，用於抑制循環和捷徑邊緣，以及一個連續抽象場（CAF）損失，該損失沿著可學習的軸在歐幾里得空間中嵌入抽象級別。<lb/>這是第一種將層級抽象形式化為標準歐幾里得嵌入中的連續性質的方法，提供了一種比雙曲方法更簡單的替代方案。<lb/>我們發布了SPHERE（https://github.com/basiralab/SPHERE），這是一個用於層級關係提取的多領域基準。<lb/>我們的框架在SciERC、SciER和SPHERE上建立了新的最先進技術，在分佈外測試中，NER提高了8.08%，RE提高了5.99%。<lb/>在零樣本設置中，NER的增益達到10.76%，RE的增益達到26.2%。
2603.23136.author: Devvrat Joshi et.al.
